architecture: Runtime Governance and Operational Admissibility Platform
core_thesis: AI is controlled before it acts, or it is not controlled at all.
core_question: Should this action be operationally admissible right now?
positioning: AI execution control platform, runtime governance layer, operational admissibility engine, and executive advisory environment for cyber and AI governance decisions before consequence.

integrated_rollout.v12: QA and stabilization posture with performance guardrails
integrated_rollout.v13: Governance Memory for saved decisions, unresolved gaps, and owner history
integrated_rollout.v14: Report depth with copy/download report actions
integrated_rollout.v15: Framework mapping for CMMC/NIST, NIST CSF, HIPAA, SOC 2, AI governance, vendor risk, and recovery validation
integrated_rollout.v18: Pilot readiness with scope, validation criteria, known limitations, and pilot brief

critical_ux_rule: Every visible executive object that appears as a chip, metric, card, gate, node, report, status, or control must either explain itself on hover/focus or navigate somewhere useful. No dead bubbles.
critical_architecture_rule: CyberShield must remain consolidated and performant. Do not reintroduce old phase-file module sprawl, broad MutationObserver loops, dynamic script injection, or timestamp-based runtime loading.
onboarding_target: About two minutes. Five minutes is the upper bound.
onboarding_steps: Human in the loop: first name, organization, primary executive owner, first report audience
Role lens: primary executive decision lens
Operating environment: industry, business value at risk, primary executive goal
Evidence and ownership: evidence confidence, workflow maturity, AI use today, owner clarity
Dashboard shape: Executive, Security, Operations, or Board
current_runtime_model: Single-file static GitHub Pages prototype with inline HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Runtime Control Center remains the center of gravity. Legacy phase modules are not loaded by the live page.
runtime_capabilities: Role, audience, organization, value-at-risk, AI posture, owner clarity, and dashboard-shape context generated through onboarding
AI action request with admissibility decision
Operational trust, evidence, policy, authority, and consequence scoring
Runtime governance feed using browser localStorage

reports_model.right_pane: Audience, recommended owner, and next conversation context
reports_model.owner_assignment: Dynamic by report type and onboarding context, not hardcoded to one role

important_boundaries: The current public build is a lightweight static prototype.
The current public build is not connected to live enterprise telemetry.
The current public build is not a production SIEM, EDR, GRC, identity, cloud control, CRM, or agent enforcement platform.
CyberShield should not be represented as fully deployed live runtime enforcement until integrations exist.
CyberShield shows decision rationale traces, not private hidden chain-of-thought.
cybershield_is: proof engine for Maximum Justice Cybersecurity advisory services
trust_principles: Trust must be modeled, evidenced, assigned, governed, and reviewed.
AI is controlled before it acts, or it is not controlled at all.
AI does not own the decision; a named human authority remains accountable.
No dead bubbles: every meaningful executive object must explain itself or navigate.
performance_guardrails: No separate version files that mutate the same DOM.
No floating drawers as primary UX.
Every feature must pass tab-switch responsiveness testing before adding the next feature.
commercial_entry_wedge: CyberShield Readiness Review or Operational Trust Assessment led by Maximum Justice Cybersecurity and Dr. Max Justice, vCISO, Security SME, Cybersecurity SME, U.S. veteran, and creator of The CHN vCISO GPT powered by Cyber Shield.
